runTitle: Local dev turbopack trace - dashboard cold compile follow-up

completedImprovements.title: Defer DashboardContent subtree during dev bootstrap
completedImprovements.note: DashboardHeader/DashboardContent switched to client-only dynamic loading and DashboardContent render is deferred by one frame in development.

completedImprovements.title: Avoid server-data graph import on dashboard dev path
completedImprovements.note: getOTelDashboardData moved into the production-only branch so dev bootstrap no longer statically imports the OTel server-data loader.

pendingImprovements.title: Trace instrumentation/layout/provider compile bottleneck
pendingImprovements.note: With curl timeout raised to 120s, turbopack trace still reports /dashboard first response only at 134s total (~37s ready + ~97s dashboard response), so the main bottleneck remains unresolved.

notes: Trace run after both dashboard deferral and page import split completed with total readiness 134s (http 200) and trace file size about 180MB.

The first dashboard response remains in the ~95-100s class after the server becomes ready, so the primary bottleneck is still inside the late dashboard compile path rather than the simple page-level server-data import.
WSL2/NTFS local dev cold compile remains the highest operator risk in the tested scope.
